sourceMovie.title: Faster
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Faster for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. George Tillman Jr. keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Rookie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Faster clicked for you, The Rookie (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Faster?
faq.answer: Try The Rookie, Drive, Transporter 3—each shares DNA with Faster while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Faster?
faq.answer: Faster (2010) is best described as Crime, Drama, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Faster worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
